input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML (`ItemsPage.xaml`)**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

    <RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
                 IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

        <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                        SelectionMode="Single"
                        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                        SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}">
            <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
                <DataTemplate>
                    <Grid Padding="10" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                        <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                        <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="{Binding Status}"
                               TextColor="Gray" />
                    </Grid>
                </DataTemplate>
            </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>

            <CollectionView.EmptyView>
                <Label Text="No items found."
                       HorizontalOptions="Center"
                       VerticalOptions="Center" />
            </CollectionView.EmptyView>
        </CollectionView>
    </RefreshView>
</ContentPage>
```

**ViewModel (`ItemsViewModel.cs`)**

```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ItemModel? _selectedItem;

    public ObservableCollection<ItemModel> Items { get; } = new();

    public ItemsViewModel(IApiService api) => _api = api;

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items.Clear();
            foreach (var item in data)
                Items.Add(item);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false;   // stops the spinner
        }
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task SelectionChangedAsync()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(
            $"{nameof(DetailPage)}",
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = SelectedItem });

        SelectedItem = null;        // clear so the same row can be re-selected
    }
}
```

### Key binding relationships

| XAML Property | ViewModel Member | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `IsRefreshing` | `IsRefreshing` (bool) | Two-way: drives spinner; set `false` to stop it |
| `Command` on `RefreshView` | `RefreshCommand` | Generated by `[RelayCommand]` on `RefreshAsync` |
| `ItemsSource` | `Items` (ObservableCollection) | Collection auto-updates the UI on add/remove |
| `SelectedItem` | `SelectedItem` | Two-way: MAUI sets it on tap; you null it after navigation |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | `SelectionChangedCommand` | Fires when selection changes; triggers navigation |

### Things to watch for

- **Set `IsRefreshing = false`** in a `finally` block â€” if you forget, the spinner never stops.
- **Null out `SelectedItem`** after navigating so the user can tap the same row again.
- `SelectionMode="Single"` gives you one-at-a-time selection; use `Multiple` + `SelectedItems` if you need multi-select.
- The ViewModel uses **CommunityToolkit.Mvvm** source generators (`[ObservableProperty]`, `[RelayCommand]`). Add the `CommunityToolkit.Mvvm` NuGet package.
